lesson: Consider two blocks that are resting one on top of the other. The upper block has mass m1 = 2.5 kg. The lower block has mass m2 and is resting on a frictionless table. Suppose the coefficient of static friction between the two blocks is given by mu_s = 0.40. A horizontal force of magnitude F = 20 N is applied to the lower block as shown. What is the minimum value of m2, in kilograms, for which the two blocks move together without slipping? Take g = 9.8 m/s^2.
